title: tests: fix diff cmd test on macOS HFS+, fixes #8860
body: If we touch too quickly after file creation, it would not noticably update the timestamp.

step_plan.summary: Fixes macOS HFS+ flakiness in `borg diff` command tests by making timestamp-dependent assertions robust: ensure filesystem timestamps actually advance between operations (touch, delete/recreate, chmod) before creating archives and checking `borg diff` output.

steps.operation: Add a filesystem-tick wait helper and use it around delete/recreate+chmod operations to guarantee mtime/ctime advance before creating the next archive and asserting time-diff output
steps.reason: On macOS HFS+ (and other coarse timestamp filesystems), operations done within the same timestamp granularity can keep identical mtime/ctime, making `borg create` record equal times in both archives and causing `borg diff` to omit the expected `{mtime}` / `{ctime}` changes. Replacing/augmenting fixed sleeps with a poll-until-stat-changes approach makes the test deterministic while keeping it fast when the FS tick advances quickly.
steps.side_effects: Slightly increases test runtime in worst-case (up to the helper timeout) but should reduce flakiness. Must ensure the helper has a bounded timeout and emits a clear failure message if timestamps don’t advance. May alter exact sequencing assumptions in the test; verify assertions still match `borg diff` output across platforms.

steps.operation: Ensure the 'touch-only' modification in the basic diff test results in a distinct timestamp by waiting for the FS tick (or explicitly sleeping/polling) before/after `Path.touch()` and before creating the second archive
steps.reason: `Path('input/file_touched').touch()` can be too fast after file creation or prior operations, producing no observable mtime/ctime change on coarse-granularity filesystems. The test expects `[cm]time:` changes for the touched file (unless `--content-only`), which will be missing if the timestamp didn’t advance; enforcing a tick change prevents intermittent failures.
steps.side_effects: May add small conditional delay/polling to the test, with minimal impact on overall suite time. Needs to avoid making content-change scenarios flaky (i.e., keep assertions focused on time-only change and ensure the tick-wait doesn’t inadvertently modify file content). If implemented via polling, ensure the helper doesn’t depend on platform-specific stat fields beyond mtime/ctime.
